evidence.text: Excellent internal tools for career growth and planning. Extensive collection of internal courses. Opportunities available for all experience levels. Offices located in over 55 countries. International relocation assistance and benefits. Strong company culture. Ideal company for career growth and learning

EPAM has a very strong engineering-driven culture with high technical standards and best practices. Great opportunities to work on complex, real-world projects for well-known international clients. evidence.text: I didn't encounter any disadvantages working here. My 5+ years of experience with EPAM

Project allocation is not always flexible (limitations by title, location, or internal rules) Being in a specific location does not guarantee access to stronger or more interesting projects Career and salary growth can be slow Compensation is around mid-market or slightly below, with limited indexation Salary increase process can be challenging Good learning base, average compensation
no real time implementation of learning bonuses are not present Ok company
Company is huge so get a raise or promotion is not easy. Good growth opportunity
Salary is not competitive at all Great opportunity to learn for low amount of money

However, you are a slave to clients. No clients support you during onboarding or the delivery of the project. Everyone is here to help you. No concept of teamwork No line manager is supportive, nor is anyone from the client side. Toxic environment. No promotion. THE WORST COMPANY

It's getting worse since 2023. Salary is not competitive. There are no relocations. There are no offline community events, only boring online ones. There are no business trips. The company has a skewed seniority pyramid with lots of directors who are toxic, incompetent and won't listen to people. The company doesn't respect its employees. There is a feeling of isolation and lack of vision or common goal. You are con...

Salary below market, and no options to get a higher salary even if you perform well in a project they force you to do the extra job on another projects in order to get a higher salary. If youâre lucky enough to get assigned to a good project with good people, youâll work without stress. But if you end up on a project with a toxic team or management, it becomes hell and no one will actually care, they just preten...
Nepotism, favoritism, low salary, project-lotto (can get great project, but the majority are waste), close to 0 company event, empty offices and losey promises from the management. mixed feelings
